title: chenopodium virgatum var. minimum
intro: Chenopodium virgatum var. minimum, also known as the lesser wormwood or dwarf lamb's-quarters, is a subspecies of the Chenopodium virgatum plant, which belongs to the family Amaranthaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant. Chenopodium virgatum var. minimum has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, due to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting detoxification. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas and infusions, have been used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
